content: Working memory capacity limits that constrain an agent’s reasoning are not a bug to be engineered away, but a built-in filtering mechanism that can drive more effective curiosity-driven active learning by forcing the agent to only prioritize encoding high-uncertainty, high-surprise information that improves future planning. This creates a natural 'curiosity bottleneck' that aligns memory constraints with active learning's goal of selecting only the most valuable new data for long-term retention.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
